name: Crisp-Fried Vegetarian Noodles
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat 1 gallon water in a large pan until boiling. Add noodles and cook until just tender, about 5 minutes. Drain.
2. Toss the noodles with 1-2 teaspoons sesame oil.
3. Immediately transfer the noodles to a round cake pan or pie plate. Press the top to level the noodles and let cool. This can be made ahead of time and refrigerated.
4. Meanwhile, cut leeks and rehydrated mushroom caps into fine julienne strips. Discard mushroom stems. Rinse and drain bean sprouts.
5. When the noodles have cooled, combine broth, rice wine, sugar, 1 teaspoon sesame oil, and cornstarch in a bowl. Blend well.
6. Heat a well-seasoned wok or skillet until very hot. Add 1 1/2 tablespoons vegetable oil and heat until nearly smoking.
7. Invert the noodle cake into the pan and cook until deep-golden brown, swirling the pan occasionally to keep the cake from sticking. Flip the noodle cake over and brown the other side.
8. Transfer the noodles to a heat-proof serving platter and keep warm in the oven at 200 degrees.
9. Reheat the pan and add the remaining 1 1/2 tablespoons vegetable oil. Again, heat until very hot.
10. Add garlic and ginger-root and stir-fry until fragrant, about 10 seconds.
11. Add mushrooms, carrots, and leeks. Stir-fry over high heat for about 1 minute.
12. Add rice wine and cook for one more minute.
13. Then add the sauce mixture and bring to boil.
14. Add bean sprouts and cook, stirring constantly to prevent lumps, until the sauce is thickened.
15. Spoon the vegetables over the fried noodle cake and serve immediately.
